revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町三日ノ浦郷字松ヶ崎313 ...'
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町三日ノ浦郷字松ヶ崎313 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|57|36.2|N|129|03|22.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大物主命 外2柱 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不祥 |本殿 = |別名 = 松ヶ崎権現 |札所等 = |例祭 =10月30日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]]三日ノ浦（みかのうら）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大物主命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主命]]、[[金山彦命]]の2柱を[[配祀]]する。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不祥。古くは社号を'''山王権現社'''としていたが、'''三日山王'''、'''蛭子神社'''とも称されたとされる。また、当社は荒川郷・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社としており、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察できる。
[[明治]]7年（[[1875年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]に列せられ、同14年（[[1882年]]）に'''[[金刀比羅神社]]'''と改称された。 [[ファイル:IMG_mika1.JPG|240px|thumb|社殿]]
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
== その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する相河郷に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、今里郷に'''[[大山祇神社(新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_ aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町三日ノ浦郷字松ヶ崎313 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|57|36.2|N|129|03|22.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大物主命 外2柱 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不祥 |本殿 = |別名 = 松ヶ崎権現 |札所等 = |例祭 =10月30日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]]三日ノ浦（みかのうら）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大物主命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主命]]、[[金山彦命]]の2柱を[[配祀]]する。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不祥。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は荒川郷・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。
[[明治]]7年（[[1875年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]に列せられ、同14年（[[1882年]]）に'''[[金刀比羅神社]]'''と改称された。 [[ファイル:IMG_mika1.JPG|240px|thumb|社殿]]
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
== その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する相河郷に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、今里郷に'''[[大山祇神社(新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_ aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町三日ノ浦郷字松ヶ崎313 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|57|36.2|N|129|03|22.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大物主命 外2柱 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不祥 |本殿 = |別名 = 松ヶ崎権現 |札所等 = |例祭 =10月30日 |神事 = }}

{{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町三日ノ浦郷字松ヶ崎313 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大物主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 松ヶ崎権現 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月30日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]][[三日ノ浦郷]]（みかのうら）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大物主命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主命]]、[[金山彦命]]の2柱を[[配祀]]する。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は[[荒川郷]]・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。
[[明治]]7年（[[1875年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]とされ、同14年（[[1882年]]）に'''金刀比羅神社'''と改称された。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
; 主な祭礼・神事 * [[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
{{-}} == その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部

== 歴史 == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は[[荒川郷]]・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。
[[明治]]7年（[[1875年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]とされ、同14年（[[1882年]]）に'''金刀比羅神社'''と改称された。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
; 主な祭礼・神事 * [[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
{{-}} == その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{citation needed:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)]][[Category:qq]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長崎県}}[[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

; 主な祭礼・神事 * [[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
{{-}} == その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>

'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]][[三日ノ浦郷]]（みかのうら）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大物主命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主命]]、[[金山彦命]]の2柱を[[配祀]]する。
== 歴史 == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は[[荒川郷]]・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。
[[明治]]7年（[[1875年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]とされ、同14年（[[1882年]]）に'''金刀比羅神社'''と改称された。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
; 主な祭礼・神事 * [[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）

また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>

{{神社 ||name=金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|native_name = 金刀比羅神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[画像:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町三日ノ浦郷字松ヶ崎313 |location = {{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = 大物主命 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 松ヶ崎権現 |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 10月30日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]][[三日ノ浦郷]]（みかのうら）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== deity == [[大物主命]]を主deityに、[[事代主命]]、[[金山彦命]]の2柱を[[配祀]]する。
== 歴史 == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は[[荒川郷]]・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。
[[明治]]7年（[[1875年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]とされ、同14年（[[1882年]]）に'''金刀比羅神社'''と改称された。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
; 主な祭礼・神事 * [[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
{{-}} == その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部

{{神社 ||name=金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|native_name = 金刀比羅神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[画像:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町三日ノ浦郷字松ヶ崎313 |location = {{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = 大物主命 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 松ヶ崎権現 |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 10月30日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]][[三日ノ浦郷]]（みかのうら）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== deity == [[大物主命]]を主deityに、[[事代主命]]、[[金山彦命]]の2柱を[[配祀]]する。
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は[[荒川郷]]・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。
[[明治]]7年（[[1875年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]とされ、同14年（[[1882年]]）に'''金刀比羅神社'''と改称された。
== Rites == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
; 主な祭礼・神事 * [[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
{{-}} == その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部

[[明治]]7年（[[1875]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]とされ、同14年（[[1882]]）に'''金刀比羅神社'''と改称された。
== Rites == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
; 主な祭礼・神事 * [[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
{{-}} == その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） File: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>

'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]][[三日ノ浦郷]]（みかのうら）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。

[[明治]]7年（[[1875]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]とされ、同14年（[[1882]]）に'''金刀比羅神社'''と改称された。
== Rites == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。

{{-}} == その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） File: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>

== deity == [[大物主命]]を主deityに、[[事代主命]]、[[金山彦命]]の2柱を[[配祀]]する。
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は[[荒川郷]]・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。

== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は[[荒川郷]]・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。

{{Shinto Shrine ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|image=[[File:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|type=Former unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Minamimatsuura District|ja|南松浦郡}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|新上五島町}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|ja|1875}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|ja|1882}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{citation needed|Kotohira Shrine}}

{{Shinto Shrine ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|image=[[File:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|type=Former unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Minamimatsuura District|ja|南松浦郡}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|新上五島町}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|ja|1875}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|ja|1882}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{citation needed|Kotohira Shrine}}

{{-}}

In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{citation needed|Kotohira Shrine}}

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Minamimatsuura District|ja|南松浦郡}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|新上五島町}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|ja|1875}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|ja|1882}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{citation needed|Kotohira Shrine}}

{{Shinto Shrine ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|image=[[File:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}} |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=draft}}) |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type=Former unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|12=draft}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamimatsuura District|ja|南松浦郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|新上五島町|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=draft}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=draft}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=draft}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=draft}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=draft}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|12=draft}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|ja|1875|12=draft}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|12=draft}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|ja|1882|12=draft}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=draft}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=draft}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=draft}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=draft}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=draft}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=draft}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=draft}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=draft}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{citation needed|Kotohira Shrine}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagasaki Prefecture}}

{{Shinto Shrine ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|image=[[File:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}} |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=draft}}) |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type=Former unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|12=draft}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= }}

== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=draft}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=draft}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=draft}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=draft}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|12=draft}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|ja|1875|12=draft}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|12=draft}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|ja|1882|12=draft}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=draft}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=draft}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=draft}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=draft}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=draft}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=draft}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=draft}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=draft}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{citation needed|Kotohira Shrine}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|明治|" to "|明治|1=Meiji era|"
7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷) up to rev 64499652
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_mika.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町三日ノ浦郷字松ヶ崎313 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大物主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 松ヶ崎権現 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月30日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]][[三日ノ浦郷]]（みかのうら）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大物主命]]を主祭神に、[[事代主命]]、[[金山彦命]]の2柱を[[配祀]]する。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代は不詳。古くは社号を「'''山王権現社'''」としていたが、「'''三日山王'''」または、事代主命・金山彦命の[[合祀]]後は「'''蛭子神社'''」と称されたとされる。また、当社は[[荒川郷]]・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]を総社とし、荒川・山王山の西の支峰に鎮座することから、その[[分霊]]を奉祀したと推察される。
[[明治]]7年（[[1875年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]とされ、同14年（[[1882年]]）に'''金刀比羅神社'''と改称された。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
; 主な祭礼・神事 * [[例大祭]]（11月24、25日）
{{-}} == その他の神社 == その他の三日ノ浦郷の神社に'''山神社'''がある。
また隣接する[[相河郷]]に'''[[姫神社]]'''が、[[今里郷]]に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_aiko.JPG|姫神社（相河郷） 画像: IMG_imazato.JPG|大山祇神社（今里郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:日吉神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長崎県}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646490]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image=File:IMG mika.JPG |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type=Former Unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} |coordinates={{Coord|32.963111|N|129.052222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|Minamimatsuura District]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō]], and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|qq=|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]].
On May 5, [[:en:1875|1875]] (the 7th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ([[:en:1882|1882]]) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|qq=|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|qq=|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|qq=|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|qq=|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagasaki Prefecture}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646490]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image=File:IMG mika.JPG |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type=Former Unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates={{Coord|32.963111|N|129.052222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|Minamimatsuura District]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō]], and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|qq=|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]].
On May 5, [[:en:1875|1875]] (the 7th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ([[:en:1882|1882]]) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|qq=|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|qq=|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|qq=|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>

== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646490]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image=File:IMG mika.JPG |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type=Former Unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates={{Coord|32.963111|N|129.052222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|Minamimatsuura District]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō]], and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]].
On May 5, [[:en:1875|1875]] (the 7th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ([[:en:1882|1882]]) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch

== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.

In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]].
On May 5, [[:en:1875|1875]] (the 7th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ([[:en:1882|1882]]) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|Minamimatsuura District]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō]], and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]].
On May 5, [[:en:1875|1875]] (the 7th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ([[:en:1882|1882]]) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagasaki Prefecture}}

In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646490]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image=File:IMG mika.JPG |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type=Former Unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates={{Coord|32.963111|N|129.052222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|Minamimatsuura District]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō]], and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]].
On May 5, [[:en:1875|1875]] (the 7th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ([[:en:1882|1882]]) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch

== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|Minamimatsuura District]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō]], and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]].
On May 5, [[:en:1875|1875]] (the 7th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ([[:en:1882|1882]]) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]]-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|en|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|WD=Q1156377|lt=Minamimatsuura District}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagasaki Prefecture}}

; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
