{{Infobox religious building| ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image=File:IMG mika.JPG |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}} |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|type=Former Unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates={{Coord|32.963111|N|129.052222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|en|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|WD=Q1156377|lt=Minamimatsuura District}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|en|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|WD=Q1156377|lt=Minamimatsuura District}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch

; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.

{{Infobox religious building| ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image=File:IMG mika.JPG |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}} |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|type=Former Unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} |coordinates={{Coord|32.963111|N|129.052222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|en|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|WD=Q1156377|lt=Minamimatsuura District|1=Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434673}}'''.

{{Infobox religious building| ||name=Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image=File:IMG mika.JPG |location=313 Matsugasaki, Mikano-ura, Shinkamigotō, Minamimatsuura District, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}} |location={{coordinates|32|57|47.2|N|129|03|08.0|E|scale:25000}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|deity=Ōmononushi ({{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|type=Former Unranked shrine ({{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}) |Foundation=Unknown (不詳) |Main sanctuary= |Alias=Matsugasaki Gongen (松ヶ崎権現) |Sacred Sites= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|festival=October 30 (10月30日) |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} |coordinates={{Coord|32.963111|N|129.052222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (Kotohira-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|en|Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki|qid=Q1156377|lt=Minamimatsuura District|1=Minami-Matsuura District, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|qid=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, and {{ill|Mikano-ura|ja|三日ノ浦郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikano-ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11358430}}.
== Deity == It enshrines {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines two deities, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, through {{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}.
== History == [[File:IMG_mika1.JPG|180px|thumb|Shrine building]] The founding date is unknown. In ancient times, the shrine was known by the name '''Sannō Gongen-sha''', but after either being called '''Mikka Sannō''' or following the joint enshrinement of Kotoshironushi and Kanayamahiko, it came to be known as '''Ebisu Shrine'''. Moreover, as this shrine served as the head shrine for {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659212}}, and was enshrined on a subsidiary peak to the west of the Arakawa/Sannō mountains, it is presumed that it also enshrined their {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}.
On May 5, {{ill|1875|en|1875|qid=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} (the 7th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era), it was classified as an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and in the 14th year ({{ill|1882|en|1882|qid=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}}) it was renamed '''Kotohira Shrine'''.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located in the former {{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11358430}}, due to the fact that the priests of {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}}-less shrines at {{ill|Arakawa District|ja|荒川郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Yodake Hie Shrine|ja|雄嶽日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodake Hie Shrine|1=Otakehie Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659212}} are in charge, there is no offering of {{ill|Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja|上五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 24–25)
{{-}}
== Other Shrines == Among the other shrines in Mikano-ura, there is a '''Yama Shrine'''. In addition, in the adjacent {{ill|Aikawa District|ja|相河郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Hime Shrine|ja|姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Shrine|qq=}}''', and in {{ill|Imazato District|ja|今里郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imazato District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上五島町|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11358430}} there is a '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi Shrine|lt_ja=大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434673}}'''.
<gallery> File: IMG_aiko.JPG|Hime Shrine (Aikawa District) File: IMG_imazato.JPG|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Imazato District) </gallery>
== References == *『{{ill|Shinkamigotō|ja|上五島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigotō|1=Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11358430}} Local History, Heisei 16』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education *『Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō』 – Shinkamigotō Board of Education, Heisei 3 *『Goto Shrine Chronicles, Showa 18 (Reprint Edition)』 – Shingi Association, Minamimatsuura District Branch
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagasaki Prefecture}}
